descriptionByLocale.it: The North Face EMEA è alla ricerca di un eccezionale Analista PSS per unirsi al nostro team con sede a Stabio, Svizzera. Il focus di questo ruolo sarà sul canale Retail Partner e Concession, con l’obiettivo principale di creare e implementare nuovi modi di lavorare per elevare la nostra pianificazione e trading di merce, allineati con il business DTC. Sarai responsabile del trading di merce, inclusa la gestione di tutto l’inventario e dei KPI Retail, oltre all’allineamento e al monitoraggio delle performance cross-funzionali per il go-to-market. Inoltre, giocherai un ruolo chiave nel rafforzare ulteriormente il nostro business Retail Partner in Italia e nella regione GAS, collaborando strettamente con i nostri manager Retail e Concession durante le campagne di vendita. Come farai la differenza: - Guidare e sviluppare un processo di Merchandising Planning digitale di livello eccellente, pienamente allineato con la nostra strategia DTC, il posizionamento e la strategia stagionale di merchandising; - Gestione completa delle performance dell’assortimento definito, collaborando con un team cross-funzionale dedicato a B&M (Buyer e Retail Partner manager); - Collaborare con il nostro team di acquisti TNG e i manager PSS per gestire i requisiti di intake stagionali, mensili e settimanali; - Lavorare sulla gestione dell’inventario e sull’ottimizzazione della copertura a livello di prodotto/taglia e tra i nostri DC, con focus sulle performance di trading e sull’ottimizzazione dei livelli di stock, rispettando gli obiettivi di The North Face B&M e finanziari; - Allineamento completo con il calendario di go-to-market per garantire un’esecuzione senza soluzione di continuità e una crescita accelerata; - Collaborare con i team di Buying, PSS e Sales Ops nello sviluppo di piani finanziari di merchandising annuali e stagionali su Ricavi, COGS, Sconti, Margini, ASP e Unità; - Stabilire standard di processi di trading e acquisto e condividere le migliori pratiche con i nostri team cross-funzionali, proponendo continuamente miglioramenti in processi e strumenti in collaborazione con i colleghi DTC e il team centrale VF; - Ricercare costantemente innovazione e ottimizzazione dei metodi di lavoro contribuendo alla creazione di nuovi processi, strumenti e report. Cosa serve per avere successo? - Oltre 3 anni di esperienza rilevante in Merchandising Planning, trading o ruolo equivalente di Business Planning/Trading; - Competenze linguistiche: fluente in inglese e italiano; - Esperienza rilevante in Merchandising Planning e analisi in un contesto retail dinamico; - Profonda comprensione dell’ambiente B&M e dei fattori di successo del merchandising per i Retail; - Ottima conoscenza di tutti i KPI Retail e del loro impatto sui P&L dei negozi; - Eccellente conoscenza di Excel. Conoscenza di Access, SAP e sistemi BI è un plus; - Spiccate capacità analitiche; - Ottima capacità di analisi commerciale e finanziaria; - Mentalità orientata al cliente, data-driven e brand-driven; - Elevata performance e orientamento ai risultati, team player che porta a termine le attività; - Maturità nella gestione della complessità organizzativa e comunicazione efficace; - Capacità strategiche e operative decisionali. Una formazione formale e una laurea triennale o magistrale in ambiti correlati sono preferibili, ma ci interessa maggiormente la tua esperienza complessiva e i tuoi risultati professionali. Cosa c’è in palio per te? - Ruolo chiave nell’accelerare la crescita B&M di uno dei nostri marchi principali; - Unirsi a un team ambizioso di Retail e Concession integrato in The North Face e VF, una delle più grandi organizzazioni multi-brand a livello globale con ottime opportunità di carriera a lungo termine; - Ambiente di lavoro internazionale, stimolante, divertente e con scopo. Siamo nel business di liberare il potenziale umano, guidati dalle idee, dall’energia e dall’impegno delle nostre persone. Per questo offriamo benefici completi che promuovono il benessere mentale, fisico e finanziario di tutti gli associati VF. Quando si tratta di benefici, siamo il pacchetto completo. - Cultura di feedback supportiva, dove rispetto e integrità guidano le nostre azioni; - Ambiente internazionale inclusivo, dove persone di background, stili di vita e nazionalità diverse amano lavorare insieme; - Essere parte di un marchio lifestyle iconico in un’organizzazione multi-brand e multi-paese; - Palestra in loco con iniziative per la salute e il benessere; - Sconto del 50% su tutti i marchi VF; - Free to Be, Inclusion & Diversity. Come azienda con scopo e orientamento alla performance, ci impegniamo a promuovere una cultura di appartenenza basata su rispetto, connessione, apertura e autenticità. Siamo dedicati a costruire e mantenere un ambiente di lavoro che celebri la diversità dei nostri collaboratori, permettendo loro di portare il proprio sé autentico ogni giorno. Unisciti alla famiglia The North Face The North Face nasce dall’amore per l’outdoor e dal desiderio di permettere a tutti di esplorare, ovunque, dalla propria casa fino all’Himalaya. Negli ultimi 50 anni abbiamo vissuto secondo il nostro “vero nord”, la convinzione che l’esplorazione abbia il potere di cambiare, sfidarci e aiutarci a vedere il mondo da nuove prospettive. Per alimentare un movimento globale di esplorazione outdoor, amore per la natura e impegno a proteggerla, accogliamo e incoraggiamo nuovi appassionati. Estendiamo il nostro raggio d’azione supportando organizzazioni no-profit, creando programmi che collegano i partecipanti a opportunità di ricreazione vicino a casa e ispirando giovani leader a esplorare e conservare. Entrando nel nostro sesto decennio, ci impegniamo a: - Potenziare l’esplorazione e il brivido dell’ignoto per più persone possibile; - Proteggere i luoghi in cui viviamo, giochiamo e operiamo; - Evolvere il nostro modo di produrre migliorando le performance ambientali e la responsabilità sociale nella supply chain. La nostra azienda madre, VF Corporation VF è una delle più grandi aziende mondiali di abbigliamento, calzature e accessori, connettono le persone agli stili di vita, alle attività e alle esperienze che apprezzano attraverso il nostro portafoglio di marchi outdoor, active e workwear. Al centro del nostro percorso c’è il nostro scopo aziendale: promuoviamo movimenti di stili di vita sostenibili e attivi per il miglioramento delle persone e del pianeta. Questo è il nostro scopo. È il motivo per cui veniamo al lavoro ogni giorno. È un impegno condiviso dai nostri collaboratori globali di tutti i marchi. Il nostro scopo ci unisce e ci guida nel perseguire i nostri obiettivi, insieme. Per saperne di più su vfc.com Se ti piace quello che hai letto e vuoi unirti al nostro team, ci piacerebbe sentirti! Per favore,
descriptionByLocale.de: The North Face EMEA sucht einen herausragenden PSS-Analysten zur Verstärkung unseres Teams mit Sitz in Stabio, Schweiz. Der Schwerpunkt dieser Rolle liegt auf dem Retail-Partner- und Concession-Kanal, mit dem Hauptziel, neue Arbeitsweisen zu entwickeln und umzusetzen, um unsere Warenplanung und den Handel im Einklang mit dem DTC-Geschäft zu verbessern. Sie sind verantwortlich für den Warenhandel, einschließlich aller Bestandsmanagement- und Retail-KPIs sowie für die funktionsübergreifende Markteinführungskoordination und Leistungsüberwachung. Außerdem spielen Sie eine Schlüsselrolle bei der weiteren Stärkung unseres Retail-Partner-Geschäfts in Italien und der GAS-Region, indem Sie eng mit unseren Retail- und Concession-Managern während der Verkaufsaktionen zusammenarbeiten. So machen Sie den Unterschied: - Leitung und Weiterentwicklung eines erstklassigen digitalen Merchandising-Planungsprozesses, vollständig auf unsere DTC-Strategie, Positionierung und saisonale Merchandising-Strategie ausgerichtet; - Vollständige Verantwortung für die Performance des definierten Sortiments, enge Zusammenarbeit mit einem funktionsübergreifenden Team, das sich B&M (Einkäufer und Retail-Partner-Manager) widmet; - Zusammenarbeit mit unserem TNG-Einkaufsteam und PSS-Managern zur Steuerung saisonaler, monatlicher und wöchentlicher Anforderungen an die Warenaufnahme; - Arbeiten an Bestandsmanagement und Coverage-Optimierung auf Produkt-/Größeneinheit und DCs, mit Fokus auf Trading-Performance und Lagerbestandsoptimierung, Erfüllung der finanziellen und B&M-Ziele von The North Face; - Vollständige Abstimmung mit dem Go-to-Market-Kalender für eine nahtlose Umsetzung und starkes, beschleunigtes Wachstum; - Zusammenarbeit mit Buying, PSS und Sales Ops bei der Entwicklung jährlicher und saisonaler Warenfinanzpläne für Umsatz, COGS, Rabatte, Margen, ASP und Einheiten; - Festlegung von Standards für Trading- und Einkaufsprozesse sowie Best Practices, proaktive Vorschläge zur kontinuierlichen Verbesserung der Prozesse und Tools in Zusammenarbeit mit DTC-Partnern und dem VF-Zentralteam; - Kontinuierliche Innovation und Optimierung der Arbeitsweisen durch die Entwicklung neuer Prozesse, Tools und Berichte. Was Sie benötigen, um erfolgreich zu sein: - Mehr als 3 Jahre relevante Berufserfahrung im Merchandising Planning, Trading oder in einer vergleichbaren Business Planning-/Trading-Rolle; - Sprachkenntnisse: fließend in Englisch und Italienisch; - Relevante Erfahrung im Merchandising Planning und in Analysen im schnelllebigen Einzelhandel; - Tiefgehendes Verständnis der schnelllebigen B&M-Umgebung und der Erfolgskriterien im Merchandising für den Einzelhandel; - Hervorragende Excel-Kenntnisse. Kenntnisse in Access, SAP und BI-Systemen sind ein Plus; - Ausgezeichnete analytische Fähigkeiten; - Hervorragendes Geschäfts- und Finanzverständnis; - Verbraucherorientierte, datenaffine und markengetriebene Denkweise; - Hohe Leistungsorientierung und Ergebnisorientierung, Teamplayer, der Dinge vorantreibt; - Reife im Umgang mit organisatorischer Komplexität und effektive Kommunikation; - Strategische und operative Entscheidungsfähigkeit. Eine formale Ausbildung und ein Bachelor- oder Master-Abschluss in verwandten Bereichen sind wünschenswert, aber wir legen mehr Wert auf Ihre Gesamterfahrung und berufliche Erfolge. Was Sie erwartet: - Eine Schlüsselrolle bei der Beschleunigung unseres B&M-Wachstums innerhalb einer unserer führenden Marken; - Beitritt zu einem ambitionierten Retail- und Concession-Team, eingebettet in The North Face und VF, eine der weltweit größten Multi-Brand-Organisationen mit großem langfristigem Karrierespektrum; - Ein internationales, herausforderndes, spaßiges Arbeitsumfeld mit Sinn. Wir sind im Geschäft, menschliches Potenzial freizusetzen, angetrieben von den Ideen, Energie und dem Engagement unserer Mitarbeitenden. Deshalb bieten wir umfassende Benefits, die das mentale, physische und finanzielle Wohlbefinden aller VF-Mitarbeitenden fördern. Wenn es um Benefits geht, sind wir das Gesamtpaket. - Eine unterstützende, feedbackorientierte Unternehmenskultur, in der Respekt und Integrität unser Handeln leiten; - Ein inklusives, internationales Umfeld, in dem Menschen mit unterschiedlichen Hintergründen, Lebensstilen und Nationalitäten gerne zusammenarbeiten; - Teil einer ikonischen Lifestyle-Marke in einer multi-brand- und multi-nationale Organisation sein; - Ein Fitnessstudio vor Ort mit Gesundheits- und Wellnessinitiativen; - 50 % Rabatt auf alle VF-Marken; - Free to Be, Inclusion & Diversity. Als zweckorientiertes, leistungsorientiertes Unternehmen setzen wir uns für eine Kultur der Zugehörigkeit ein, die auf Respekt, Verbundenheit, Offenheit und Authentizität basiert. Wir verpflichten uns, einen Arbeitsplatz zu schaffen und zu pflegen, der die Vielfalt unserer Mitarbeitenden feiert, sodass sie jeden Tag authentisch sein können. Werde Teil der North Face Familie The North Face wurde aus der Liebe zur Natur und dem Wunsch gegründet, allen Menschen die Möglichkeit zu geben, zu erkunden – von ihrem Garten bis zu den Himalayas. In den letzten 50 Jahren haben wir an unserem „wahren Norden“ festgehalten, dem Glauben, dass Exploration die Kraft hat, uns zu verändern, herauszufordern und uns neue Perspektiven auf die Welt zu eröffnen. Um eine globale Bewegung für Outdoor-Exploration, Naturliebe und Umweltschutz zu fördern, begrüßen und ermutigen wir neue Teilnehmer. Wir erweitern unsere Reichweite durch Unterstützung von Non-Profit-Organisationen, durch Programme, die Teilnehmer mit Naherholungsmöglichkeiten verbinden, und durch die Inspiration junger Führungskräfte, zu erkunden und zu bewahren. Im Eintritt in unser sechstes Jahrzehnt verpflichten wir uns: - Exploration und das Abenteuer des Unbekannten für möglichst viele Menschen zu fördern; - Die Orte zu schützen, an denen wir leben, spielen und arbeiten; - Unser Produktdesign weiterzuentwickeln, um unsere Umweltleistung und soziale Verantwortung in der Lieferkette zu verbessern. Unsere Muttergesellschaft, VF Corporation VF ist eines der weltweit größten Unternehmen für Bekleidung, Schuhe und Accessoires, das Menschen mit den Lebensstilen, Aktivitäten und Erfahrungen verbindet, die sie schätzen, durch unsere ikonischen Outdoor-, Active- und Workwear-Marken. Im Mittelpunkt unseres Weges steht unser unternehmensweites Ziel: Wir fördern Bewegungen nachhaltiger und aktiver Lebensstile zum Wohle der Menschen und unseres Planeten. Das ist unser Zweck. Es ist der Grund, warum wir jeden Tag zur Arbeit kommen. Es ist ein gemeinsames Engagement unserer globalen Mitarbeitenden aller Marken. Unser Zweck vereint uns und führt uns dazu, unsere Ziele gemeinsam zu verfolgen. Mehr erfahren unter vfc.com Wenn Sie das, was Sie gelesen haben, anspricht und Sie unserem Team beitreten möchten, freuen wir uns auf Ihre Bewerbung! Bitte senden Sie Ihre Bewerbung auf Englisch, da dies unsere gemeinsame Sprache ist!
descriptionByLocale.fr: The North Face EMEA recherche un analyste PSS exceptionnel pour rejoindre notre équipe basée à Stabio, en Suisse. L’objectif principal de ce poste sera le canal Retail Partner et Concession, avec pour but de créer et mettre en œuvre de nouvelles méthodes de travail afin d’élever notre planification et trading de marchandises en accord avec le business DTC. Vous serez responsable du trading de marchandises, y compris la gestion de tout l’inventaire et des KPI Retail, ainsi que l’alignement et le suivi des performances interfonctionnelles pour le lancement sur le marché. De plus, vous jouerez un rôle clé pour renforcer davantage notre activité Retail Partner en ITALIE et dans la région GAS, en travaillant en étroite collaboration avec nos managers Retail et Concession tout au long de la campagne de vente. Comment vous ferez la différence : - Diriger et faire évoluer un processus de Merchandising Planning numérique de classe mondiale, entièrement aligné avec notre stratégie DTC, notre positionnement et notre stratégie de merchandising saisonnière ; - Prendre en charge la performance de l’assortiment défini, en collaborant étroitement avec une équipe interfonctionnelle dédiée à B&M (acheteurs et managers Retail Partner) ; - Travailler aux côtés de notre équipe d’achats TNG et des managers PSS pour gérer les besoins saisonniers, mensuels et hebdomadaires en approvisionnement ; - Travailler sur la gestion des stocks et l’optimisation de la couverture au niveau des produits/taille et dans nos centres de distribution, en se concentrant sur la performance commerciale et l’optimisation des niveaux de stock, en atteignant les objectifs financiers et de B&M de The North Face ; - S’assurer d’un alignement complet avec le calendrier de mise sur le marché pour garantir une exécution fluide et une croissance accélérée ; - Collaborer avec les équipes Buying, PSS et Sales Ops pour élaborer les plans financiers annuels et saisonniers de marchandises sur le chiffre d’affaires, le COGS, les remises, les marges, l’ASP et les unités ; - Définir des standards pour les processus de trading et d’achat et partager les meilleures pratiques avec nos équipes interfonctionnelles, en proposant de manière proactive des améliorations continues des processus et des outils en collaboration avec les partenaires DTC et l’équipe centrale VF ; - Rechercher constamment l’innovation et l’optimisation des méthodes de travail en contribuant à la mise en place de nouveaux processus, outils et rapports. Que faut-il pour réussir ? - Plus de 3 ans d’expérience pertinente en Merchandising Planning, trading ou dans un rôle équivalent de Business Planning/Trading ; - Compétences linguistiques : maîtrise de l’anglais et de l’italien ; - Expérience pertinente en Merchandising Planning et analyse dans un environnement retail dynamique ; - Bonne compréhension de l’environnement B&M et des facteurs clés de succès du merchandising pour le retail ; - Excellente connaissance d’Excel. La connaissance d’Access, SAP et des systèmes BI est un plus ; - Excellentes compétences analytiques ; - Forte capacité d’analyse commerciale et financière ; - Mentalité orientée client, data-driven et axée sur la marque ; - Très orienté performance et résultats, un joueur d’équipe capable de mener à bien ses missions ; - Maturité dans la gestion de la complexité organisationnelle et communication efficace ; - Capacité stratégique et opérationnelle à prendre des décisions. Une formation formelle et un diplôme universitaire de Bachelor ou Master dans des domaines connexes sont un plus, mais nous privilégions votre expérience globale et vos réalisations professionnelles. Ce que cela vous apporte : - Jouer un rôle clé dans l’accélération de notre croissance B&M au sein de l’une de nos marques phares ; - Rejoindre une équipe Retail et Concession ambitieuse, intégrée à The North Face et VF, l’une des plus grandes organisations multi-marques au monde, offrant de belles opportunités de carrière à long terme ; - Un environnement de travail international, stimulant, amusant et porteur de sens. Nous sommes dans le business de libérer le potentiel humain, guidés par les idées, l’énergie et l’engagement de nos collaborateurs. C’est pourquoi nous proposons des avantages complets qui encouragent le bien-être mental, physique et financier de tous les collaborateurs VF. Quand il s’agit d’avantages, nous sommes le package complet. - Une culture de feedback soutenue, où le respect et l’intégrité guident nos actions ; - Un environnement international inclusif où des personnes de divers horizons, modes de vie et nationalités aiment travailler ensemble ; - Faire partie d’une marque de lifestyle emblématique dans une organisation multi-marques et multi-pays ; - Une salle de sport sur site proposant des initiatives pour la santé et le bien-être ; - 50 % de réduction sur toutes les marques VF ; - Free to Be, Inclusion & Diversity. En tant qu’entreprise guidée par un but et axée sur la performance, nous nous efforçons de favoriser une culture d’appartenance basée sur le respect, la connexion, l’ouverture et l’authenticité. Nous nous engageons à construire et maintenir un lieu de travail qui célèbre la diversité de nos collaborateurs, leur permettant d’être eux-mêmes chaque jour. Rejoignez la famille The North Face The North Face est née d’un amour pour l’extérieur et du désir de permettre à tous de découvrir, du jardin de leur maison jusqu’à l’Himalaya. Au cours des 50 dernières années, nous avons vécu selon notre « vrai nord », la conviction que l’exploration a le pouvoir de nous changer, de nous défier et de nous aider à voir le monde sous de nouvelles perspectives. Pour encourager un mouvement mondial d’exploration en plein air, d’amour pour la nature et d’engagement à la protéger, nous accueillons et encourageons de nouveaux participants. Nous étendons notre influence en soutenant des organisations à but non lucratif, en créant des programmes qui connectent les participants à des activités de loisirs proches de chez eux, et en inspirant de jeunes leaders à explorer et à préserver. En entrant dans notre sixième décennie, nous nous engageons à : - Favoriser l’exploration et le frisson de l’inconnu pour le plus grand nombre ; - Protéger les lieux où nous vivons, jouons et opérons ; - Faire évoluer notre façon de fabriquer nos produits en améliorant nos performances environnementales et notre responsabilité sociale dans la chaîne d’approvisionnement. Notre société mère, VF Corporation VF est l’une des plus grandes entreprises mondiales de vêtements, chaussures et accessoires, connectant les gens à leurs modes de vie, activités et expériences chéris à travers notre famille de marques emblématiques outdoor, active et workwear. Au cœur de notre parcours se trouve notre raison d’être : nous dynamisons des mouvements de modes de vie durables et actifs pour le bien des personnes et de notre planète. C’est notre raison d’être. C’est la raison pour laquelle nous venons au travail chaque jour. C’est un engagement partagé par nos collaborateurs mondiaux de toutes les marques. Notre raison d’être nous unit et nous pousse à poursuivre nos objectifs ensemble. Pour en savoir plus, visitez vfc.com Si vous avez aimé ce que vous avez lu et souhaitez rejoindre notre équipe, nous aimerions avoir de vos nouvelles ! Veuillez soumettre votre candidature en anglais, car c’est notre langue commune !

jobs.title: Head of Product Development and Materials
jobs.location: EMEA · CHE · Stabio · VF Campus VF1
jobs.description: Napapijiri is looking for an exceptional Head of Product Development and Materials to join our Product team based in Stabio, Switzerland. For over 30 years, Napapijri has pioneered the future of fashion. From our Italian beginnings, innovating outdoor apparel in the shadow of Monte Bianco, to tackling nature’s most challenging environments on polar expeditions, our desire to lead the way is what makes us who we are. Let’s Talk about the Role You will oversee the apparel development organization , ensuring that seasonal brand direction, business priorities, sourcing capabilities, and quality standards are fully aligned. This position requires both strategic vision and hands-on execution, guiding the product creation calendar to consistently deliver against design intent, quality, margin, and timeline expectations. You will inspire and lead your team , providing coaching and guidance to drive departmental and brand success. As Director of Product Development and Materials, you will collaborate closely with leaders in Design, Merchandising, Sourcing, and Innovation to maximize efficiency in calendar adherence, margin performance, product quality, and delivery outcomes. Beyond execution, you will play a pivotal role in shaping business growth by developing product category strategies across seasons and channels, aligned with brand objectives, sourcing capabilities, and quality standards. Partnering with key stakeholders, you will identify opportunities to expand commercial growth through a comprehensive and balanced product offering. How You Will Make a Difference You will achieve this by: - Drive product category strategies by season and channel, aligned with business objectives and growth targets. - Build, lead, and manage Product Development and Material strategies, ensuring effective implementation across teams and vendors. - Define and manage the operating budget for the department, balancing innovation with cost efficiency. - Partner with leaders in Design, Merchandising, Sourcing, Innovation, Operations, and Commercial functions to maximize efficiency, margin, quality, and delivery rates. - Implement and monitor product development programs, including raw material commercialization, fit, calendar, and color adherence. - Lead brand product innovation through continuous research into new materials, technologies, and product functionalities. - Drive the creative cycle by providing trend guidelines to Design, Material, and Merchandising teams. - Ensure accuracy and integrity of product specifications throughout the development process. - Understand and analyze global market trends, shifts, drivers, and downturns to inform product strategies. - Provide insights and feedback to Product, Merchandising, Sales, and Marketing teams to continuously improve product plans and go-to-market tools. - Leverage the brand DNA of style and sustainability to meet relevant consumer trends and expectations. - Ensure team compliance with seasonal timeline milestones, recommending and sharing calendar adjustments as needed. - Manage the seasonal product workflow in alignment with GTM timelines - Collaborate with sourcing teams on vendor allocation, pricing challenges, and production issues to optimize outcomes. - Deliver targets in gross margin, efficiency, effectiveness, and cost savings during development and GTM stages. - Lead, coach, and develop Product Development and Materials teams (10+ members), fostering collaboration and high performance. - Unlock and build digital product creation processes - Champion a culture of innovation, accountability, and continuous improvement. Skills for Success - Extended and solid experience in Product Development in a medium or large sized apparel company - Strong knowledge of product development processes including design, costing, manufacturing and sourcing, preferably with Asian based production and cut/sewn category - Strong project planning and management skills, ability to develop and manage multiple timelines and deadlines - Experience with Product Data Management systems (ie. Enovia) preferred - Creates a strong, collaborative and supportive work culture where people can achieve objectives and fulfil their job expectations - Strong project planning and management skills, ability to develop and manage multiple timelines and deadlines - Creates a strong, collaborative and supportive work culture where people can achieve objectives and fulfil their job expectations What’s in it For You We’re in the business of unleashing human potential, driven by the ideas, energy and commitment of our people. That’s why we offer comprehensive benefits that encourage mental, physical and financial well-being for all VF associates. When it comes to benefits, we’re the total package. - A supportive feedback-based culture where respect and integrity guide us in what we do - An inclusive international environment where people of diverse backgrounds, lifestyles and nationalities love working together - Be part of an iconic lifestyle brand in a multi brand, multi countries organization - On site gym offering health and well-being initiatives - A discount card with 50% on all VF brands - Break out areas offering complimentary hot drinks Free to Be, Inclusion & Diversity As a purpose‑led, performance‑driven company, we foster a culture of belonging based on respect, openness, connection, and authenticity. We celebrate diversity and support equal opportunities for all associates and applicants. R-20260325-0013

jobs.title: Retail Auditor Intern - EMEA Internship Program
jobs.location: EMEA · CHE · Stabio · VF Campus VF1
jobs.description: Be a dreamer, make the difference! Join #VFInternshipProgram in our EMEA HQ in Stabio, Switzerland as Retail Auditor, Intern for Finance Team. About VF VF is redefining what it means to be the world’s best apparel, footwear and equipment company, powering iconic active lifestyle brands including The North Face®, Vans® and Timberland®. Learn more at http://vfc.com . Key information - Start date: June 1st, 2026 - Duration: 12 months , full-time - Gross monthly salary: 1’850 CHF - Location: VF EMEA Headquarters in Stabio, Switzerland - Streamlined recruitment process: 3 interviews (Recruiter, Hiring Manager, potential Business Case) Eligibility requirements - You must be graduating after May 1st, 2026 OR you must have a student status as of June 2026 - You must hold EU or EEA (European Economic Area) citizenship Let’s talk about the role! Are you interested in supporting a Retail Audit team and taking on an exciting challenge within the dynamic store environment? In this role, you will: - Support database preparation for the launch of new claim procedures. - Contribute to AI literacy by supporting the integration of AI concepts into existing processes and procedures to improve efficiency where possible. - Audit inventory movements , ensuring accuracy between store records and accounting data. - Assist team members with their daily operational and audit activities. How you will make a difference Skills that will make you succeed: - Fluency in English , spoken as well as written; any other European language like French or German is a plus - Strong working knowledge of computer systems, especially Microsoft Office and Outlook , with a dvanced Excel skills being essential. Exposure to AI tools is a plus. - Excellent planning, analytical, and organizational skills - Ability to work independently while also collaborating effectively within a team What’s in it for you At the end of the Internship period, you can expect to have a clear understanding of the below: - Learning & Stock Monitoring : Support daily inventory activities by learning how to monitor stock levels and reporting any immediate discrepancies. - Data Collection & Analysis Support : Assist the team in gathering data to investigate inventory mismatches and help map out potential root causes (e.g., entry errors or damages). - Operational Support for Counts : Actively participate in periodic stock takes and cycle counts, ensuring physical counts match system records under supervision. - Cross-functional Collaboration : Support information flow between Supply Chain, Logistics, and Warehouse teams to maintain data accuracy. - Basic Reporting & Dashboards : Help prepare simple inventory reports and update tracking dashboards (e.g., using Excel or internal tools). - Process Contribution : Provide "fresh-eye" observations to identify small operational bottlenecks and suggest simple improvements to daily workflows. The VF EMEA Internship Program is a 12-month journey designed to develop and empower emerging talents at the start of their careers. We are committed to unleashing your potential, so you can expect a structured learning path and rich hands-on experience , including: - Dedicated skill-building workshops & internal consulting group project - One day of work in-store gaining real insights about our brands - Networking opportunities with peers and senior leadership In this internship, you’ll gain hands-on experience in how seasonal assortments are built and brought to life across the European market. You’ll work closely with experienced merchandisers, gaining exposure to product strategy, consumer insights, and cross-functional collaboration. We cannot grant that the internship will lead you to a contract afterwards, but thanks to the value of the experience most of our interns are staying in the company after the internship period. Since we want you to focus on what matters, we offer comprehensive benefits that encourage mental, physical, and financial well-being. You will enjoy: - Hybrid working policy - 50% discount on VF brands - On site gym, offering health and well-being initiatives - Subsidized canteen, as well as break out areas offering complimentary drinks - Shuttle bus to the office or discount on public transport, depending on your location - A thriving interns’ community, with people of diverse backgrounds, lifestyles, and nationalities who love working together. Free to Be, Inclusion & Belonging As an equal opportunity employer, we strive to foster a culture of belonging based on respect, connection, openness and authenticity. We are committed to building and maintaining a workplace that celebrates the diversity of our associates, allowing them to bring their authentic selves to work every day. Are you in? Apply now – early application increases your chances! #readytoapply R-20260327-0005

descriptionByLocale.it: Be a dreamer, make the difference! Join #VFInternshipProgram in our EMEA HQ in Stabio, Switzerland as Retail Auditor, Intern for Finance Team. About VF VF is redefining what it means to be the world’s best apparel, footwear and equipment company, powering iconic active lifestyle brands including The North Face®, Vans® and Timberland®. Learn more at http://vfc.com . Key information - Start date: June 1st, 2026 - Duration: 12 months , full-time - Gross monthly salary: 1’850 CHF - Location: VF EMEA Headquarters in Stabio, Switzerland - Streamlined recruitment process: 3 interviews (Recruiter, Hiring Manager, potential Business Case) Eligibility requirements - You must be graduating after May 1st, 2026 OR you must have a student status as of June 2026 - You must hold EU or EEA (European Economic Area) citizenship Let’s talk about the role! Are you interested in supporting a Retail Audit team and taking on an exciting challenge within the dynamic store environment? In this role, you will: - Support database preparation for the launch of new claim procedures. - Contribute to AI literacy by supporting the integration of AI concepts into existing processes and procedures to improve efficiency where possible. - Audit inventory movements , ensuring accuracy between store records and accounting data. - Assist team members with their daily operational and audit activities. How you will make a difference Skills that will make you succeed: - Fluency in English , spoken as well as written; any other European language like French or German is a plus - Strong working knowledge of computer systems, especially Microsoft Office and Outlook , with a dvanced Excel skills being essential. Exposure to AI tools is a plus. - Excellent planning, analytical, and organizational skills - Ability to work independently while also collaborating effectively within a team What’s in it for you At the end of the Internship period, you can expect to have a clear understanding of the below: - Learning & Stock Monitoring : Support daily inventory activities by learning how to monitor stock levels and reporting any immediate discrepancies. - Data Collection & Analysis Support : Assist the team in gathering data to investigate inventory mismatches and help map out potential root causes (e.g., entry errors or damages). - Operational Support for Counts : Actively participate in periodic stock takes and cycle counts, ensuring physical counts match system records under supervision. - Cross-functional Collaboration : Support information flow between Supply Chain, Logistics, and Warehouse teams to maintain data accuracy. - Basic Reporting & Dashboards : Help prepare simple inventory reports and update tracking dashboards (e.g., using Excel or internal tools). - Process Contribution : Provide "fresh-eye" observations to identify small operational bottlenecks and suggest simple improvements to daily workflows. The VF EMEA Internship Program is a 12-month journey designed to develop and empower emerging talents at the start of their careers. We are committed to unleashing your potential, so you can expect a structured learning path and rich hands-on experience , including: - Dedicated skill-building workshops & internal consulting group project - One day of work in-store gaining real insights about our brands - Networking opportunities with peers and senior leadership In this internship, you’ll gain hands-on experience in how seasonal assortments are built and brought to life across the European market. You’ll work closely with experienced merchandisers, gaining exposure to product strategy, consumer insights, and cross-functional collaboration. We cannot grant that the internship will lead you to a contract afterwards, but thanks to the value of the experience most of our interns are staying in the company after the internship period. Since we want you to focus on what matters, we offer comprehensive benefits that encourage mental, physical, and financial well-being. You will enjoy: - Hybrid working policy - 50% discount on VF brands - On site gym, offering health and well-being initiatives - Subsidized canteen, as well as break out areas offering complimentary drinks - Shuttle bus to the office or discount on public transport, depending on your location - A thriving interns’ community, with people of diverse backgrounds, lifestyles, and nationalities who love working together. Free to Be, Inclusion & Belonging As an equal opportunity employer, we strive to foster a culture of belonging based on respect, connection, openness and authenticity. We are committed to building and maintaining a workplace that celebrates the diversity of our associates, allowing them to bring their authentic selves to work every day. Are you in? Apply now – early application increases your chances! #readytoapply R-20260327-0005

jobs.title: Associate, Merchandising Operations (Timberland)
jobs.location: EMEA · CHE · Stabio · VF Campus VF1
jobs.description: Timberland EMEA is looking for an exceptional Associate, Merchandising Operations on a 6‑month fixed‑term contract , to join our team based in Stabio, Switzerland. ## Let’s Talk about the Role As Associate, Merchandising Operations , you will support and partially own key operational activities across Global Merchandising, Global Product, Regional Merchandising, Planning, Pricing and Commercial teams. You will contribute to ensuring accuracy, consistency and timely delivery of Global Merchandising deliverables, while helping streamline templates, calendars, systems and processes to improve efficiency across the merchandising organization. You will contribute by: • Enovia/PLM System Coordination - Maintain and monitor data accuracy within Enovia/PLM across categories. - Act as a point of contact for basic-to-intermediate PLM issues, ensuring timely escalation and resolution with the Merch Ops Manager and system teams. - Support system enhancements and new tool/process adoption (UAT testing, documentation, data updates). - Identify recurring issues or gaps and propose improvements to data flows or operational routines. • Line Plan Communication and Management - Coordinate pre‑season and in‑season distribution of line lists, ensuring updates reach all cross‑functional partners according to calendar. - Collaborate with Global Merchandising, Regional Merchandising, Planning, Pricing, Master Data and Commercial teams to ensure product information accuracy and timely system flow into SAP. - Support troubleshooting for SKU‑level issues and syndication delays, driving first-level analysis before escalation. • Merchandising Operations & Process Support - Maintain and update global merchandising calendars, templates and timelines. - Help ensure process adherence across merchant teams by monitoring deadlines and tracking key milestones. - Prepare and maintain process documentation, input materials, dashboards and reports used by the Merch Ops function. - Contribute to identifying areas for process improvement and support implementation of new ways of working. - Represent Merch Ops in selected cross-functional meetings when required, ensuring clear communication of deliverables and follow-ups. • Merch Analytics & Reporting - Support category and merchandising teams with ad‑hoc reports, structured data pulls and preliminary insights. - Coordinate catalogue development tasks, ensuring timely collection and validation of product information. - Maintain updated merchandising databases and ensure accessibility of information for internal and external stakeholders. Skills for Success The ideal Associate, Merch Ops has strong analytical skills, attention to detail, and a collaborative mindset, with growing autonomy and confidence in managing operational workflows. - A few years of experience in Merch Ops, Merchandising, Planning, Business Operations or similar roles - Strong Excel skills and good understanding of PLM systems - Solid understanding of GTM process and basic retail/wholesale mechanics - Familiarity with merchandising planning concepts and product creation is a strong plus - Ability to organize information, manage multiple deadlines and support decision‑making - Strong communication and stakeholder management skills - Fluent in English (written and verbal) - Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Business, Economics, Engineering, Apparel or related fields ## Free to Be, Inclusion & Diversity As a purpose‑led, performance‑driven company, we foster a culture of belonging based on respect, openness, connection, and authenticity. We celebrate diversity and support equal opportunities for all associates and applicants. If this sounds like you and you’re ready to join our team, we’d love to hear from you! #readytoapply R-20260309-0029

jobs.title: Developer, Apparel
jobs.location: EMEA · CHE · Stabio · VF Campus VF1
jobs.description: Timberland EMEA is looking for an exceptional Developer, Apparel , to join our team based in Stabio, Switzerland. ## Let’s Talk about the Role As a key member of our Product Development team, you will provide insights and suggestions and share information to deliver quality products on time at the right cost for the intended performance use. Your work will be performed under general guidance and direction, which may require the initiation of contacts to resolve and discuss mutual problems or collaboration on procedures or transactions. Infrequent overseas development travel may be necessary. ## How You Will Make a Difference - Develop product across multiple genders by product category depending on the seasonal style count and expertise. - Participate meetings and execute against the Product expectations for product quality, cost (predictive costing) - Own Product specifications, measurements, BOM, Fit creations and maintenance. - Communicate effectively and clearly with Asia sourcing & vendors, TD/ GPC / GSP - Collaborate w/ design & merch to support the line plan through product execution and signing of for bulk production. - Hit required product creation milestones and execute on strategy - Work w/ Global Product costing team in VFA and internal merch/ design teams on seasonal FOBs to support Gross margin growth. - Be an engaged and committed team-mate, Be a facilitator in negotiations. ## ## Skills for Success The foundation skills you will need in this position are: - The ability to work with cross-functional teams, including Consumer Research, Research and Development to compile and evaluate research on future product requirements and identify enhancements to current products - The aptitude to help the Product Development team establish strategies ranging from product pricing, design, marketing, advertising, packaging, and future products to manage the product family's life - An understanding and knowledge of Timberland’s principles, practices, and procedures - The resourcefulness to determine a course of action based on guidelines and modify processes and methods as required - The capability to resolve issues which are often varied and non-routine - Apply previous experience to identify the most appropriate option or improve existing approaches - Seek clarification when needed - Undertake analysis and investigation to solve differing, but related, problems - The talent to convey complex information to occasionally divergent audiences that may require some persuasion - In some instances, audiences are not knowledgeable of the subject matter - Or an objective review of difficult work problems, obtaining cooperation or approval ## While formal education in a related field is great to have, we are most interested in a few years of experience and professional achievements. ## What’s in it For You - A supportive, feedback‑based culture guided by respect and integrity. - An inclusive, international environment where diverse backgrounds and experiences are valued. - The opportunity to be part of an iconic lifestyle brand within a global, multi‑brand organization. - On‑site gym with health and well‑being initiatives. - A discount card offering 50% off all VF brands. - Break‑out areas with complimentary hot drinks. About Timberland At Timberland, we’re a force of nature. We’re a team that steps outside, works hard together, and moves the world forward. The brand is widely recognized as the arbiter of boot culture. With a rich heritage of craftsmanship and an eye toward the future, Timberland has a decades-long commitment to make products responsibly, protect the outdoors, and build community. ## Free to Be, Inclusion & Diversity As a purpose‑led, performance‑driven company, we foster a culture of belonging based on respect, openness, connection, and authenticity. We celebrate diversity and support equal opportunities for all associates and applicants. If this sounds like you and you’re ready to join our team, we’d love to hear from you! R-20260323-0023

Participate meetings and execute against the Product expectations for product quality, cost (predictive costing) - Own Product specifications, measurements, BOM, Fit creations and maintenance. - Communicate effectively and clearly with Asia sourcing & vendors, TD/ GPC / GSP - Collaborate w/ design & merch to support the line plan through product execution and signing of for bulk production. - Hit required product creation milestones and execute on strategy - Work w/ Global Product costing team in VFA and internal merch/ design teams on seasonal FOBs to support Gross margin growth. - Be an engaged and committed team-mate, Be a facilitator in negotiations. ## ## Skills for Success The foundation skills you will need in this position are: - The ability to work with cross-functional teams, including Consumer Research, Research and Development to compile and evaluate research on future product requirements and identify enhancements to current products - The aptitude to help the Product Development team establish strategies ranging from product pricing, design, marketing, advertising, packaging, and future products to manage the product family's life - An understanding and knowledge of Timberland’s principles, practices, and procedures - The resourcefulness to determine a course of action based on guidelines and modify processes and methods as required - The capability to resolve issues which are often varied and non-routine - Apply previous experience to identify the most appropriate option or improve existing approaches - Seek clarification when needed - Undertake analysis and investigation to solve differing, but related, problems - The talent to convey complex information to occasionally divergent audiences that may require some persuasion - In some instances, audiences are not knowledgeable of the subject matter - Or an objective review of difficult work problems, obtaining cooperation or approval ## While formal education in a related field is great to have, we are most interested in a few years of experience and professional achievements. ## What’s in it For You - A supportive, feedback‑based culture guided by respect and integrity. - An inclusive, international environment where diverse backgrounds and experiences are valued. - The opportunity to be part of an iconic lifestyle brand within a global, multi‑brand organization. - On‑site gym with health and well‑being initiatives. - A discount card offering 50% off all VF brands. - Break‑out areas with complimentary hot drinks. About Timberland At Timberland, we’re a force of nature. We’re a team that steps outside, works hard together, and moves the world forward. The brand is widely recognized as the arbiter of boot culture. With a rich heritage of craftsmanship and an eye toward the future, Timberland has a decades-long commitment to make products responsibly, protect the outdoors, and build community. ## Free to Be, Inclusion & Diversity As a purpose‑led, performance‑driven company, we foster a culture of belonging based on respect, openness, connection, and authenticity. We celebrate diversity and support equal opportunities for all associates and applicants. If this sounds like you and you’re ready to join our team, we’d love to hear from you! R-20260323-0023

jobs.title: Analyst, FP&A
jobs.location: EMEA · CHE · Stabio · VF Campus VF1
jobs.description: VF Corporation is looking for an exceptional FP&A Analyst , Shared Functions to join our Finance team based in Stabio, Switzerland. Let’s talk about the role! As FP&A Analyst, will support brand FP&A activities related to P&L Planning and Forecasting focusing on Sales & Margin of Wholesale channel, playing a key role to create alignment around Gross-To-Net, analysing the main channel and accounts dynamics and performances as well as supporting the brand to make the right decisions to maximize marginality. You will gather information on forecast & plan data, which has to be loaded in the reporting tool (BPC) and deliver reports, as determined by area leads and Senior Management. To achieve the goals of the department you will work closely together with your colleagues of the Finance department as well as other functions such as DTC, Planning, Sales and report directly to the Director, FP&A. We expect that you will deliver unbeatable results whilst fulfilling the following aspects of the FP&A Analyst role: - Manage and handle Financial GTN planning and Forecasting tools and ensure consistency and accuracy of figures submitted in the systems; - Ensure that actuals are aligned to expectations and proactively analyse and report variances vs forecast with - Keep continuous alignment between financial & commercial GTN to ensure alignment/balance between Sales, Margin and inventory targets; - Deliver forecasting accuracy excellence and pro-actively analyze any critical trends or dynamics within the channels and the main accounts; - Ensure that all recurring requests related to forecast and plan are delivered accurately and on time: store roll, key accounts details, revenue walk, margin bridge, category overviews, etc.; - Support the 5YR rolling financial plan (LRP) partnering with the global strategy, global merch planning & Ops and Product. Understand the brand strategy and support in drawing conclusions through financial analysis; - Assist in ad-hoc analysis on request of European or Global/Corporate management Skills for Success You will be a professional with proven experience in business controlling and financial planning, with a strong analytical mindset and: - Minimum bachelor’s degree in finance area (accounting, controlling) and 1/2 years of related work experience preferrable; - Solid software and PC skills (Advanced in Excel, PowerPoint, SAP/COGNOS, BPC experience is a plus) - Great time management and prioritizing skills with the ability to meet strict deadlines - Strong English command (written and verbal) - Proven communication skills in interacting with people from a non-financial background - Excellent organizational skills & goal-oriented About VF VF is a global apparel company connecting people to the lifestyles they cherish through our family of iconic brands. At the heart of our journey lies our purpose: We power movements of sustainable and active lifestyles for the betterment of people and our planet. This is our purpose. It’s the reason we come to work every day. Our purpose unites us and leads us to pursue our goals, together. This is our calling. Learn more at vfc.com What’s in it For You We’re in the business of unleashing human potential, driven by the ideas, energy and commitment of our people. That’s why we offer comprehensive benefits that encourage mental, physical and financial well-being for all VF associates. When it comes to benefits, we’re the total package. - A supportive feedback-based culture where respect and integrity guide us in what we do - An inclusive international environment where people of diverse backgrounds, lifestyles and nationalities love working together - Be part of an iconic lifestyle brand in a multi brand, multi countries organization - On site gym offering health and well-being initiatives - A discount card with 50% on all VF brands - Break out areas offering complimentary hot drinks Free to Be, Inclusion & Diversity As an equal opportunity employer, we strive to foster a culture of belonging based on respect, connection, openness and authenticity. We are committed to building and maintaining a workplace that celebrates the diversity of our associates, allowing them to bring their authentic selves to work every day. R-20260323-0032

jobs.title: Allocator Intern - EMEA Internship Program
jobs.location: EMEA · CHE · Stabio · VF Campus VF1
jobs.description: Be a dreamer, make the difference! Join #VFInternshipProgram in our EMEA HQ in Stabio, Switzerland as Allocator Intern for Vans. About VF VF is redefining what it means to be the world’s best apparel, footwear and equipment company, powering iconic active lifestyle brands including The North Face®, Vans® and Timberland®. Learn more at http://vfc.com . Key information - Start date: June 1st, 2026 - Duration: 12 months , full-time - Gross monthly salary: 1’850 CHF - Location: VF EMEA Headquarters in Stabio, Switzerland - Streamlined recruitment process: 3 interviews (Recruiter, Hiring Manager, potential Business Case) Eligibility requirements - You must be graduating after May 1st, 2026 OR you must have a student status as of June 2026 - You must hold EU or EEA (European Economic Area) citizenship Let’s talk about the role! We are looking for a Allocator intern to join VANS within VF EMEA Internship Program. In this role, you will provide support to the Allocation Team: - By taking an active role in analysing performance and sales trend. - Monitoring deliveries, maintaining adequate stock levels. - Taking care of replenishment / initial allocation for O&O EMEA Stores. - Developing and maintaining reports and tools finding the best way to make them efficient and useful for the team. How you will make a difference You should be an analytical, resilient and curious team player, being: - Fluent in English with excellent verbal and written communication skills. - Passionate for numbers with strong analytical skills: accuracy and attention to details is a must. - Proficient in Microsoft Excel. Knowledge of any BI platform will be considered as a plus (eg: Power BI). - Eager to learn and face up new challenges. - Proactive in order to achieve yours and your team goals. - First experiences such as other internships, extracurricular activities, is considered as a plus What’s in it for you At the end of the Internship period, you can expect to have a clear understanding of the below: - Learn about Vans’ trends and main logics of Retail Merchandising. - Learn logics of inventory management, sales analysis, and allocation strategies. - Learn to work with a wide team of internal stakeholder. The VF EMEA Internship Program is a 12-month journey designed to develop and empower emerging talents at the start of their careers. We are committed to unleashing your potential, so you can expect a structured learning path and rich hands-on experience , including: - Dedicated skill-building workshops & internal consulting group project - One day of work in-store gaining real insights about our brands - Networking opportunities with peers and senior leadership We cannot grant that the internship will lead you to a contract afterwards, but thanks to the value of the experience most of our interns are staying in the company after the internship period. Since we want you to focus on what matters, we offer comprehensive benefits that encourage mental, physical, and financial well-being. You will enjoy: - Hybrid working policy - 50% discount on VF brands - On site gym, offering health and well-being initiatives - Subsidized canteen, as well as break out areas offering complimentary drinks - Shuttle bus to the office or discount on public transport, depending on your location - A thriving interns’ community, with people of diverse backgrounds, lifestyles, and nationalities who love working together. jobs.title: Retail Marketing Intern - EMEA Internship Program
jobs.location: EMEA · CHE · Stabio · VF Campus VF1
jobs.description: Be a dreamer, make the difference! Join #VFInternshipProgram in our EMEA HQ in Stabio, Switzerland as Retail Marketing Intern for Vans. About VF VF is redefining what it means to be the world’s best apparel, footwear and equipment company, powering iconic active lifestyle brands including The North Face®, Vans® and Timberland®. Learn more at http://vfc.com . Key information - Start date: June 1st, 2026 - Duration: 12 months , full-time - Gross monthly salary: 1’850 CHF - Location: VF EMEA Headquarters in Stabio, Switzerland - Streamlined recruitment process: 3 interviews (Recruiter, Hiring Manager, potential Business Case) Eligibility requirements - You must be graduating after May 1st, 2026 OR you must have a student status as of June 2026 - You must hold EU or EEA (European Economic Area) citizenship Let’s talk about the role! This internship offers hands-on experiences at the intersection of marketing and retail, where you’ll help shape in-store activations while learning how brand strategy comes to life at the point of sale Support the planning and execution of retail marketing initiatives that enhance in-store visibility, customer engagement, and sales performance. Gain experience analyzing results, coordinating with cross-functional teams, and translating brand strategy into impactful retail activations. Get ready to make an impact! Here’s what you’ll be responsible for joining our team as Retail Marketing intern: - Support the planning and execution of retail marketing and in-store activations, helping bring Vans’ brand, culture, and stories to life in stores. - Coordinate with cross-functional teams (Retail, Creative, Visual Merchandising, and other Marketing functions) to gather all tactics needed to deliver engaging in-store events and consumer activations. - Assist with retail marketing operations across EMEA, supporting different store formats (Owned, Partner, Outlet). - Contribute to training and communication materials for store teams ahead of key activations or events. - Help execute retail campaigns and store activations, including in-store visuals, digital retail communications, and event-related materials. - Provide day-to-day support on cross-functional needs, such as copy coordination, translations, and event logistics. - Work closely with DTC (Direct To Consumer) Retail teams (district and store managers) to support smooth execution at store level. - Support the creation of briefs and post-activation recaps, capturing learnings and best practices for future in-store initiatives. - Support basic budget tracking and purchase order follow-up in coordination with the team. Skills that will make you successful You are a natural facilitator who helps ideas turn into real, on-the-ground experiences: - Project-minded and well-organized – You enjoy working in project mode, juggling multiple activations, and coordinating across Creative, Retail, Visual Merchandising, and Marketing teams. - Curious about retail marketing and in-store activations – You’re eager to understand how brand storytelling, consumer experience, and store execution come together in a physical retail environment. - Culturally connected – You have a strong eye for engaging content and a genuine affinity with action sports, street culture, and the Vans DNA (skate, music, art, and self-expression). - Detail-driven yet hands-on – You care about quality, follow through on execution, and take pride in getting the small details right for events and store activations. - Proactive and solution-oriented -You show initiative, adapt quickly, and keep things moving even when plans evolve or challenges arise. - Collaborative by nature – You thrive in team environments, share information openly, and contribute positively to collective success. - Comfortable working with data and tools – You enjoy using tools like Excel and PowerPoint to track projects, support reporting, and tell clear, visual stories. - A Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Marketing, Communication, Business, or a related field is nice to have, but we are most interested in your mindset, curiosity, and relevant experiences. What’s in it for you By joining the Vans Retail Marketing team, you’ll gain real, hands-on experience working on in-store activations and events for an iconic global action sports brand. During your internship, you will: - Work closely with international and cross-functional teams across Retail, Marketing, Creative, and Visual Merchandising. - Contribute to real retail activations and in-store events, from idea to execution. - Learn how brand strategy translates into physical retail experiences that engage consumers. - Receive exposure to retail marketing operations across EMEA, including different store formats. - Benefit from on-the-job learning, mentoring, and collaboration with experienced professionals in the industry. By the end of the internship, you can expect to have a clear understanding of: - How retail marketing and in-store activations are planned, executed, and measured. - How cross-functional teams collaborate to deliver impactful store experiences. - How Vans’ brand values and action-sports culture are expressed at retail level. - The operational side of retail marketing, including asset production, timelines, and budget basics. - What it takes to successfully deliver consumer-facing events in a fast-paced retail environment. The VF EMEA Internship Program is a 12-month journey designed to develop and empower emerging talents at the start of their careers. We are committed to unleashing your potential, so you can expect a structured learning path and rich hands-on experience , including: - Dedicated skill-building workshops & internal consulting group project - One day of work in-store gaining real insights about our brands - Networking opportunities with peers and senior leadership We cannot grant that the internship will lead you to a contract afterwards, but thanks to the value of the experience most of our interns are staying in the company after the internship period. Since we want you to focus on what matters, we offer comprehensive benefits that encourage mental, physical, and financial well-being. You will enjoy: - Hybrid working policy - 50% discount on VF brands - On site gym, offering health and well-being initiatives - Subsidized canteen, as well as break out areas offering complimentary drinks - Shuttle bus to the office or discount on public transport, depending on your location - A thriving interns’ community, with people of diverse backgrounds, lifestyles, and nationalities who love working together. Free to Be, Inclusion & Belonging As an equal opportunity employer, we strive to foster a culture of belonging based on respect, connection, openness and authenticity. We are committed to building and maintaining a workplace that celebrates the diversity of our associates, allowing them to bring their authentic selves to work every day. Are you in? Apply now – early application increases your chances! #readytoapply R-20260206-0013

Skills that will make you successful You are a natural facilitator who helps ideas turn into real, on-the-ground experiences: - Project-minded and well-organized – You enjoy working in project mode, juggling multiple activations, and coordinating across Creative, Retail, Visual Merchandising, and Marketing teams. - Curious about retail marketing and in-store activations – You’re eager to understand how brand storytelling, consumer experience, and store execution come together in a physical retail environment. - Culturally connected – You have a strong eye for engaging content and a genuine affinity with action sports, street culture, and the Vans DNA (skate, music, art, and self-expression). - Detail-driven yet hands-on – You care about quality, follow through on execution, and take pride in getting the small details right for events and store activations. - Proactive and solution-oriented -You show initiative, adapt quickly, and keep things moving even when plans evolve or challenges arise. - Collaborative by nature – You thrive in team environments, share information openly, and contribute positively to collective success. - Comfortable working with data and tools – You enjoy using tools like Excel and PowerPoint to track projects, support reporting, and tell clear, visual stories. - A Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Marketing, Communication, Business, or a related field is nice to have, but we are most interested in your mindset, curiosity, and relevant experiences. What’s in it for you By joining the Vans Retail Marketing team, you’ll gain real, hands-on experience working on in-store activations and events for an iconic global action sports brand. During your internship, you will: - Work closely with international and cross-functional teams across Retail, Marketing, Creative, and Visual Merchandising. - Contribute to real retail activations and in-store events, from idea to execution. - Learn how brand strategy translates into physical retail experiences that engage consumers. - Receive exposure to retail marketing operations across EMEA, including different store formats. - Benefit from on-the-job learning, mentoring, and collaboration with experienced professionals in the industry. By the end of the internship, you can expect to have a clear understanding of: - How retail marketing and in-store activations are planned, executed, and measured. - How cross-functional teams collaborate to deliver impactful store experiences. - How Vans’ brand values and action-sports culture are expressed at retail level. - The operational side of retail marketing, including asset production, timelines, and budget basics. - What it takes to successfully deliver consumer-facing events in a fast-paced retail environment. The VF EMEA Internship Program is a 12-month journey designed to develop and empower emerging talents at the start of their careers. We are committed to unleashing your potential, so you can expect a structured learning path and rich hands-on experience , including: - Dedicated skill-building workshops & internal consulting group project - One day of work in-store gaining real insights about our brands - Networking opportunities with peers and senior leadership We cannot grant that the internship will lead you to a contract afterwards, but thanks to the value of the experience most of our interns are staying in the company after the internship period. Since we want you to focus on what matters, we offer comprehensive benefits that encourage mental, physical, and financial well-being. You will enjoy: - Hybrid working policy - 50% discount on VF brands - On site gym, offering health and well-being initiatives - Subsidized canteen, as well as break out areas offering complimentary drinks - Shuttle bus to the office or discount on public transport, depending on your location - A thriving interns’ community, with people of diverse backgrounds, lifestyles, and nationalities who love working together. Free to Be, Inclusion & Belonging As an equal opportunity employer, we strive to foster a culture of belonging based on respect, connection, openness and authenticity. We are committed to building and maintaining a workplace that celebrates the diversity of our associates, allowing them to bring their authentic selves to work every day. Are you in? Apply now – early application increases your chances! #readytoapply R-20260206-0013

We are committed to unleashing your potential, so you can expect a structured learning path and rich hands-on experience , including: - Dedicated skill-building workshops & internal consulting group project - One day of work in-store gaining real insights about our brands - Networking opportunities with peers and senior leadership We cannot grant that the internship will lead you to a contract afterwards, but thanks to the value of the experience most of our interns are staying in the company after the internship period. Since we want you to focus on what matters, we offer comprehensive benefits that encourage mental, physical, and financial well-being. jobs.title: CRM & Loyalty Intern - EMEA Internship Program
jobs.location: EMEA · CHE · Stabio · VF Campus VF1
jobs.description: Be a dreamer, make the difference! Join #VFInternshipProgram in our EMEA HQ in Stabio, Switzerland as CRM & Loyalty Intern for Vans. About VF VF is redefining what it means to be the world’s best apparel, footwear and equipment company, powering iconic active lifestyle brands including The North Face®, Vans® and Timberland®. Learn more at http://vfc.com . Key information - Start date: June 1st, 2026 - Duration: 12 months , full-time - Gross monthly salary: 1’850 CHF - Location: VF EMEA Headquarters in Stabio, Switzerland - Streamlined recruitment process: 3 interviews (Recruiter, Hiring Manager, potential Business Case) Eligibility requirements - You must be graduating after May 1st, 2026 OR you must have a student status as of June 2026 - You must hold EU or EEA (European Economic Area) citizenship Let’s talk about the role! Vans is looking for a curious and driven CRM & Loyalty Intern to support how we engage, reward, and grow our consumer community across EMEA, based in Stabio, Switzerland. Support the execution of Vans’ CRM and Loyalty strategy across EMEA, contributing to how we engage our community, strengthen loyalty, and bring the Vans brand to life through meaningful, data‑driven consumer experiences. You will gain hands‑on exposure to lifecycle marketing, loyalty mechanics, and cross‑functional collaboration within a global, youth‑culture‑driven brand. - Support the execution of CRM campaigns across email, eCommerce, and other consumer touchpoints, ensuring brand consistency and flawless delivery - Contribute to the setup, monitoring, and optimization of loyalty activations to drive engagement, retention, and member value - Manage and update CRM assets such as campaign calendars, briefs, tracking files, and dashboards to support day‑to‑day execution - Be responsible for supporting reporting and post‑campaign analysis, helping translate consumer and performance data into clear insights - Develop a strong understanding of the Vans consumer, community mindset, and lifecycle stages by working closely with EMEA and Central teams - Support additional ad-hoc digital media initiatives and projects as part of the wider Vans Brand Planning team How you will make a difference What will make you successful in this role: - A strong passion for the Vans brand, youth culture, and community‑driven marketing, with curiosity about how brands build long‑term relationships - A genuine interest in CRM, loyalty programs, and data‑informed decision‑making, beyond just campaign execution - An analytical mindset, with the ability to connect performance data to consumer behavior and brand impact - High attention to detail and structure, essential for managing multiple campaigns and stakeholders in parallel - A proactive, hands‑on attitude, comfortable working in a fast‑moving, international environment and learning by doing - A degree in Marketing, Business, Economics, Data, or a related field is nice to have What’s in it for you In the role of CRM & Loyalty Intern , you will work at the heart of Vans’ consumer engagement strategy, gaining hands‑on experience in how a global youth‑culture brand builds long‑term relationships with its community. By joining our team, you will: - Gain practical experience in CRM, loyalty, and lifecycle marketing , working on real campaigns that reach millions of consumers across EMEA - Learn how to translate consumer data and insights into meaningful actions that drive engagement, retention, and brand love - Develop a strong understanding of Vans Family , loyalty mechanics, and how community‑driven brands create value beyond transactions - Work closely with an international, cross‑functional team , collaborating with Brand, Digital, eCommerce, and Central CRM teams - Build confidence using industry‑leading tools and processes , with exposure to campaign execution, reporting, and performance optimization - Benefit from a supportive learning environment, including on‑the‑job coaching, feedback, and networking opportunities across teams and brands within VF This internship is a unique opportunity to learn by doing, grow your skill set, and contribute to shaping how Vans connects with its community — always "Off The Wall". The VF EMEA Internship Program is a 12-month journey designed to develop and empower emerging talents at the start of their careers. We are committed to unleashing your potential, so you can expect a structured learning path and rich hands-on experience , including: - Dedicated skill-building workshops & internal consulting group project - One day of work in-store gaining real insights about our brands - Networking opportunities with peers and senior leadership We cannot grant that the internship will lead you to a contract afterwards, but thanks to the value of the experience most of our interns are staying in the company after the internship period. Since we want you to focus on what matters, we offer comprehensive benefits that encourage mental, physical, and financial well-being. You will enjoy: - Hybrid working policy - 50% discount on VF brands - On site gym, offering health and well-being initiatives - Subsidized canteen, as well as break out areas offering complimentary drinks - Shuttle bus to the office or discount on public transport, depending on your location - A thriving interns’ community, with people of diverse backgrounds, lifestyles, and nationalities who love working together. Free to Be, Inclusion & Belonging As an equal opportunity employer, we strive to foster a culture of belonging based on respect, connection, openness and authenticity. We are committed to building and maintaining a workplace that celebrates the diversity of our associates, allowing them to bring their authentic selves to work every day. Are you in? Apply now – early application increases your chances! #readytoapply R-20260206-0012
